biography: Own @hamptonfounders - building elite peer groups for $3M-$100M startup CEOs. Apply, will change your life! Host @myfirstmilpod. Make dumb jokes.

latestIgtvVideos.caption:
My senior year I ran 22 flat in the 200m dash. 

So close to breaking 22 seconds (good for a high schooler). 

This will forever be my uncle Rico “I use to throw a pig skin over them mountains. I should have taken state” moment. 

Smdh

A team building activity we used to do when I first started The Hustle:

- we all go to costco
- each 2 person team gets $200
- 25 minutes to shop
- goal: buy something we all can enjoy in the office
- afterwords we eat Costco pizza, chicken bake, cheese balls and vote on most creative, useful, funny. 

So much fun.

At the end of a tough soaring session.. ya boy got caught. 

Went out just for a second.

Ha!!

In Hot Springs, Arkansas. 

In the 1800s, gangsters like Al Capone, business folk like Vanderbilt, and athletes would come here to bath and drink from the hot springs. 

They say it cured syphilis and other things. 

I had to try it myself.

Syphilis free now!

I paid $50 to use a special machine to test my bodyfat.

1) This is an amazing machine. Everyone should do it.

2) The results shocked me!

Anyone else done this? What do you think?

Full video here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=w7HqQBsDwBY&t=2s
Will be able to touch the ground soon. How low can you go?!
My buddy rented a water park. The best part: the wave machine.

ANNOUNCEMENT: We’re giving away $5000 to a people who turn our pod content into cool clips.

It’s simple. Go to our YouTube, chop pods into cool side bites, post whoever using our hashtag #mfmclip. 

We will give $5k to the person with the most views and best vids.

mfmpod.com/clips
The fact that a station wagon can go this fast and a sound like this…makes me happy.
I’m thankful I live next door to @neville_medhora because he can come over while squatting and film the gains.

Reclaiming my body in my 30s. 

Trying new sports, exercising, and…doing flips and shit. 

Living a life that makes 12 year old me proud!

latestPosts.caption:
The buyer (Hubspot) had like 10-15 lawyers + accountants asking me questions. 

One of the questions, I swear, was like about a $50 purchase on Fivver. They needed to know all the vendors we used so to make sure we weren’t violated any non-solicits. 

I was like “this was a $50 logo job on Fiverr from a dude in Asia with a username bigballerboi69 — I didn’t exactly track the invoice or non-compete well on this one.”

Thankfully, they were cool with it...

But seriously, if you’re sicking of building alone and want IRL help…Hampton.

We created groups of 8 other founders for monthly convos with a trained moderator. All done IRL in your city. 

And its totally life changing (and if it isn't, you don't pay). 

But..there is a catch: you need at least $3m in revenue and have a thorough vetting process...otherwise this whole thing stinks.

Link in my bio to apply!

I sold my company when I was 31 for over $40m (sale price + performance bonuses). 

I personally netted tens of millions after taxes.

Before that? I tried 15+ businesses, all small wins, all steps toward the big one.

Talking about these numbers potentially makes me look silly. I get it. 

But I’m sharing to let other founders know: KEEP GOING. 

My current company, Hampton, is for founders doing $3M+ in revenue who want a real peer group.

We’re 1,000+ founders averaging $25 million per year, meeting monthly in person to help each other scale and stay sane.

If interested in, comment “hampton” and I’ll send you application details!

https://joinhampton.com/

PS: I have like 5-10 wins and losses. I can make a part 2 if you’re interested!

Shout out to my bud @marshallhaas. His video inspired mine. If you like this, check out him.

#startups #business #founder
We’re the Parrs and we are here to f*ck shit up.

Here’s what people who’ve built companies are reading.

What should we read next?

latestPosts.caption:
My mother in law came to America as a teen immigrant.

She was a stay at home mom for decades. 

She didn’t know how to use a computer. 

Then in her 50s she started an online pillow store.

Now she does over $1,000,000 a year. Works a few days a week. Babysits her grandkids the rest of the time.

She talked about how immigrants feel displaced and have nothing to lose, so they go harder.
That hunger is real.

We did a podcast about her story on My First Million. One of my favorites we’ve ever done. Link in bio.

It’s one of the most influential business books ever written.
The entire backstory is completely fabricated.

latestPosts.caption:
I have a simple rule. I'll only speak at conferences if my best friends @nickgraynews and @neville_medhora 

I'm so fortunate and lucky that people care enough about my opinion to invite me to speak in front of crowds. 

But I'll only go if these guys come with me and help me because it turns a work thing into a best friend trip. 

Also nick is hilarious and often takes the mic for the intro.

latestPosts.caption:
The prettiest bank lobby in NYC. Apple Bank on the Upper West Side (where I live). 

Built in 1928 for the Central Savings Bank

Modeled after an Italian Renaissance palazzo, with the ceiling inspired by the Davanzati Palace in Florence. 

The banking hall has 65-foot ceilings — taller than most churches

It’s the only freestanding bank building in Manhattan and one of the few grand banking halls still operating as an actual bank

My fav part is how they have every bank president on the wall since 1850s. 

Fun fact: this building is worth $100-150M, lol roughly 5-10% of the bank’s value.

latestPosts.caption: Sam Parr, World Renowned Pick Up Artist International Man of Mystery and Denim Aficionado, As Seen On The Mel Robbins Podcast.

I changed my mind about content production. 

I used to think that in order to succeed you needed to have the “it factor.” 

But now I think process-driven creators can kill it by having a great system and iterating. 

Who are some great process-driven creators you know?

Jacob retired from Major League Baseball at 29 with $15M. 

Many athletes who make their money young aren’t able to hold onto it for long. 

But Jacob took an incredibly low-spend approach that allowed him to preserve and grow his wealth.

I made a document with his personal finances and 50+ other successful entrepreneurs and investors. 

If you want a link to the document, comment “yes” and I’ll DM you.
